title: Norwegian Rye Fritters
description: A traditional Norwegian recipe adapted to make crispy rye fritters with inviting flavors.

ingredients.name: Fritter mix-ins
ingredients.notes: such as chopped onions, chopped fresh parsley, or crumbled feta cheese

instructions: In a large bowl, combine the rye flour, grated carrots, salt, and black pepper.
Add the buttermilk, egg, and crumbled fritter mix-ins to the bowl. Mix until a smooth batter forms.
Heat about 1/2 inch of norwegian butter in a large skillet over medium heat.
Using a 1/4 cup measuring cup, scoop the batter into the hot skillet.
Fry the fritters for about 4 minutes on each side, until they are golden brown and crispy.
Using a slotted spoon, remove the fritters from the skillet and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Serve the fritters warm, with your favorite dipping sauce or aioli.
Store leftover fritters in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 3 days.
